title: Tedingea pygmaea
intro: I couldn't find any information about the plant 'Tedingea pygmaea.'
taxonomy: Tedingea pygmaea is a species of skink, a type of lizard. It belongs to the family Scincidae, which is one of the largest and most diverse families of lizards. The genus Tedingea is a group of skinks endemic to Australia, specifically found in the eastern and northern regions. Tedingea pygmaea is a small species of lizard, with a total length of approximately 5-6 centimeters. This species is classified as a reptile, belonging to the class Reptilia and the phylum Chordata.
